title: Publish Receipt
description: One receipt per published internal artifact. Emitted at publish time and written back to the brain registry, so every AI (or human) work product has a known owner, location, version history, and audit trail. Publication is a governed runtime action, not a loose LLM output.

artifact_id.description: Stable identifier for the artifact across all its versions. Does not change on re-publish; the version field tracks each deploy.

title.description: Human-readable name, e.g. 'Liquidity Dashboard'. What a person types when searching the registry.

owner_role.description: The ROLE that owns this artifact, never a bare person name. Survives the individual leaving, e.g. 'finance-lead', 'merch-ops'.

purpose.description: One line on what the artifact is for. Enough for a stranger to know in five seconds if this is the tool they want.

sensitivity.description: Sensitivity class from the capture spine. Drives who is allowed to open the URL; not decoration.

space.description: Brain-space id that defines the viewing audience (see Brain Spaces). Access to the published URL follows this space, not a new hand-rolled list.

url.description: Company-controlled URL where the artifact is live, behind company sign-on. Never a personal hosting account or an expiring preview link.

version.description: The version this receipt records. Explicit and current after this publish; enables one-step rollback.

previous_version.description: The version that was live before this publish, or null for a first publish. The rollback target.

published_at.description: UTC timestamp of the publish action.

published_by.description: The ROLE (person or agent) that ran the publish, for the audit trail. A role, not a bare name.

source_brain_path.description: Path back into the brain to the doc/context the artifact was built from. Makes the artifact traceable, never a dead end.

rollback_path.description: How to restore the previous version in one step (e.g. a stored deploy handle for previous_version). Null on first publish.

audit_event_id.description: Id of the audit event emitted for this publish (see audit-event.schema.json). Links the receipt to the consequential-action audit trail.
